data.title: LUNR Earnings: Intuitive Machines Stock Sinks After Its Q4 Revenue Missed Wall Street Estimate
data.description:
Intuitive Machines (LUNR) stock fell on Thursday following the release of the space manufacturing and supply company's Q4 2025 earnings report. This report started with revenue of $44.79 million, which was well below the $53.71 million that Wall Street expected. Investors will also note that the company's revenue fell 18.05% year-over-year from $54.66 million.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Intuitive Machines didn't provide earnings per share data in its latest earnings report. However, the company did report a net loss of $59.66 million. That's a major improvement over the net loss of $165.15 million reported in the fourth quarter of 2024. The company attributed its shrinking net loss to the completion of its second lunar mission, its expansion into national security space programs, its acquisition of KinetX Aerospace, and other factors.

Intuitive Machines stock was down 3.87% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 4.18% fall yesterday. Even so, the stock has rallied 11.58% year-to-date and 154.71% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/19/26 – Stock Futures Slump as Iran War Intensifies, Fed Warns of Inflation
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday as the Israel-Iran war intensified, pushing oil prices higher. The U.S. Federal Reserve kept the interest rate unchanged yesterday but warned of higher inflationary pressure. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.56%, 0.42%, and 0.38%, respectively, at 5:36 a.m. EST on March 19.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 3.29% at $115.48 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) traded near $96.81 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that surging oil prices could stoke inflation fears. The Dow Jones fell to a fresh 2026 low, tumbling 1.63%, while the S&P 500 shed 1.36% and the Nasdaq dropped 1.46%.

After markets closed, Micron Technology (MU) shares fell more than 4% despite beating fiscal Q2 sales and earnings expectations. The chipmaker's revenues nearly tripled, fueled by a memory chip supply shortage, and guided above consensus for Q3. However, its increased capex guidance left investors worried about returns.

Looking ahead to economic reports, data on building permits, new home sales, weekly jobless claims, and Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index are due today.

Trade QQQ with leverage

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Alibaba (BABA), FedEx (FDX), and Accenture (ACN).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.28%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price traded around $4,720 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower amid the escalating war. Chip companies are already facing shortages as the war has halted freight routes.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on March 19 as global investors digested the impact of the Israel-Iran war.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.02%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.39%, while the Shenzhen Component declined 2.36%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei plunged 3.38%, with the Topix losing 2.91%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Is China Natural Resources Stock (CHNR) Soaring Today?
data.description:
China Natural Resources (CHNR) stock rocketed higher on Thursday after the Chinese metal mining company announced a non-binding Letter of Intent (LOI) with Feishang Group Limited. This agreement will have China Natural Resources acquire a 100% stake in a Feishang Group Limited subsidiary that will own 59.79% of the equity interest of HooRii Technology. Investors will also note that Feishang Group Limited is the principal shareholder of China Natural Resources.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Natural Resources' plans to take a stake in HooRii Technology will expand its influence beyond mining and into AI. HooRii Technology is a Chinese physical AI company focused on combining Internet of Things (IoT) and AI. China Natural Resources noted that it plans to strengthen its mining operations alongside its shift to AI, allowing it to have a dual focus.

The finer details of the acquisition agreement are still being worked out. However, the LOI includes a potential purchase price of $37 million to $40 million for the 100% stake in the Feishang Group Limited subsidiary. China Natural Resources intends to pay this via a mix of cash and stock issuance. The company didn't provide a timeframe for when the deal would be completed.

China Natural Resources Stock Rallies Today

China Natural Resources stock was up 66.06% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 1.49% rally yesterday. The shares have fallen 8.33% year-to-date and 28.89% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of CHNR stock, as more than 16.6 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 42,000 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Launches $293 Million Genesis Mission to Drive Next AI and Quantum Wave
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Energy has launched a $293 million funding call to support its Genesis Mission, a program that links AI with key science fields, including quantum tech. The plan invites teams from labs, firms, and schools to use AI models to solve more than 20 national issues. These areas include quantum systems, energy, biotech, and advanced building tools. As a result, the move shows how the U.S. is tying AI and quantum computing into a broader tech plan.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Darío Gil, Under Secretary for Science, said the goal is to drive new ideas across sectors. He noted that the mission aims for "breakthrough ideas and novel collaborations" across public and private groups. In turn, this reflects a push to speed up progress by linking talent from many fields.

Funding Structure and Timeline

The program will fund both short and long-term work. Phase I awards range from $500,000 to $750,000 for nine-month projects. Meanwhile, Phase II awards range from $6 million to $15 million over three years. Teams can apply to either phase in 2026, and Phase I winners may move on to larger awards later.

New trading tool for QUBT bulls/bears

Key dates are set for the next steps. Phase I bids and Phase II intent letters are due April 28, 2026. Full Phase II bids are due May 19, 2026, with a webinar planned for March 26.

Overall, the plan fits into a broader trend in which the U.S. backs AI-led research tied to quantum and other core technologies. For investors, this type of funding may help shape early-stage work and guide how future tools move from lab use to real-world use.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align notable quantum stocks to gain a broader perspective of the industry. In the list, you can find pure-play stocks such as Rigetti (RGTI) and Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT), and larger-caps with quantum operations such as Alphabet (GOOGL) and Microsoft (MSFT).
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Micron (MU) Stock Drops Despite Strong Earnings — Top Analysts Split on What Comes Next
data.description:
Micron (MU) is down about 7% in early trading on Thursday, extending losses from the previous session, despite reporting a strong fiscal Q2 results, as investors react to higher spending plans and a mixed outlook. The company posted earnings of $12.20 per share on revenue of $23.9 billion, both well ahead of expectations, but the reaction suggests the rally may be starting to cool.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AI Demand Drives Strong Results

Micron's results clearly showed how strong the memory cycle is right now. Revenue nearly tripled from a year ago, while margins and free cash flow hit record levels, driven by AI demand and tight supply across the industry.

Management said demand remains strong, especially for high-bandwidth memory, and supply is still constrained. In fact, the company noted that some customers are not getting all the memory they need, highlighting just how tight the market is.

But none of this came as a surprise. Investors already expected a strong quarter, and the focus has now shifted from current demand to how long these favorable conditions can last.

Spending Plans Raise New Questions

The bigger shift in sentiment came from Micron's investment outlook. The company now expects fiscal 2026 capital spending to exceed $25 billion and signaled an even larger step-up in 2027, as it builds capacity to meet AI demand.

That is where investors are getting cautious. While the spending is needed to support long-term growth, it also raises concerns about how sustainable current margins are once supply starts catching up.

Management also hinted that today's tight supply environment will not last forever. Over time, as capacity comes online, pricing gains are expected to normalize — something the market is already starting to factor in.

Top Analysts Are Divided on the Stock's Next Move

  • Top Bernstein analyst Mark Li raised his price target to $510, citing Micron's strong guidance and continued AI momentum.
  • Goldman Sachs 5-star analyst Toshiya Hari maintained a Buy rating, calling the higher CapEx an "offensive move" to capture more share in AI memory.
  • Piper Sandler analyst Harsh Kumar reiterated an Overweight rating, saying the pullback could be a buying opportunity given ongoing supply tightness.
  • However, Summit Insights analyst Kinngai Chan downgraded the stock to Hold, warning that pricing gains could slow and stock outperformance may moderate in the second half of FY26.

Micron's results confirm that the AI-driven memory cycle remains strong. But with expectations already high and spending rising, investors are starting to question how much upside is left.

Is Micron a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Micron stock based on 24 Buy and two Hold recommendations. The average 12-month MU stock price target of $473 indicates 2.44% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Accenture Stock (ACN) Slides on Middle East Warning despite Q2 Beat, Record Bookings
data.description:
Shares in Accenture (ACN) dropped about 4% early Thursday after the professional services company provided fiscal 2026 guidance that took cognizance of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The outlook overshadowed its Wall Street-beating Q2 2026 results, which came with record second-quarter bookings of $22.1 billion.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bookings jumped 6% year-over-year based on current USD exchange rates (that is, in U.S. dollars), fueled by growth in Accenture's efforts to help organizations adopt enterprise AI to improve their operations.

Accenture Hails 'Strong AI-Driven Growth'

During the three-month period that ended on February 28, Accenture saw its adjusted earnings per share rise by about 4% to $2.93, ahead of the Wall Street consensus of $2.84. Its revenue also jumped by 8% YoY in U.S. dollars to $18 billion, surpassing analysts' consensus estimate of $17.84 billion.

However, on a local currency basis — that is, excluding the impact of exchange-rate fluctuations — its new bookings only rose 1%, while revenue climbed by a much smaller 4%.

"We're accelerating our critical work with clients to scale advanced AI across their enterprise, and we're seeing strong AI-driven growth," noted Julie Sweet, Accenture's chair and CEO.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Quantum Computing News: New Study Questions Scale as U.S. and UK Pour Billions Into Growth
data.description:
Welcome to Thursday's update on quantum news. This round shows a mix of views on how far quantum tech can scale, along with fresh public funding and new work on hybrid systems. At the same time, regulators in Europe have begun to examine how the market may evolve as the tech moves closer to real-world use.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

1. New Study Questions How Far Quantum Can Scale

First, a new study in PNAS suggests that quantum systems may face a hard cap on size. The work presents a model called Rational Quantum Mechanics, which claims that quantum systems have a fixed data limit.

In turn, the study estimates that useful systems may top out at about 200-1,000 qubits. Beyond that point, the system may lose its edge over classic computing tools.

This view also has a clear impact on cyber risk. The study says a system strong enough to break 2048-bit RSA may not be possible at all. As a result, some forms of data code may stay safe due to basic limits in physics, not just slow tech progress.

Still, the idea is not yet proven. The author notes that tests on larger systems in the next few years may help determine whether the limit is real.

2. U.S. Backs AI and Quantum with $293 billion Plan

Next, the U.S. Department of Energy has launched a $293 million funding call tied to its Genesis Mission. The plan aims to use AI to help solve key issues in fields such as quantum tech, energy, and biotech.

The program will fund both short and long-term work. Phase I awards range from $500,000 to $750,000, while Phase II may reach up to $15 million over three years.

Darío Gil, Under Secretary for Science, said the goal is to drive new ideas across sectors. He noted that the plan seeks "breakthrough ideas and novel collaborations" across labs, firms, and schools.

As a result, the push shows how the U.S. is linking AI and quantum work as part of a wider tech race.

3. Europe Starts Early Review of Quantum Market Risks

Meanwhile, Italy's Competition Authority has opened a review of the quantum sector. The move aims to spot risks early as the market grows.

Have high conviction in NVDA? Leverage your view

The group points to high costs, deep tech skill needs, and long build times as key entry barriers. It also flags the risk of user lock-in, since many firms build full stacks that are hard to switch.

In addition, the rise in patent filings may give early leaders a strong hold on key tech. This could shape how the market forms before it fully matures.

The review is not a probe of any one firm. Instead, it is an early look at how rules may need to adapt as quantum moves from the lab to use.

4. Classiq and NVIDIA Show Gains in Hybrid Workflows

Next, Classiq has shown new gains from its link with NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) and its CUDA-Q platform. The work focuses on hybrid systems that pair quantum code with GPU power.

In tests, a 31-qubit finance task ran much faster with this setup. The full run time fell from 67 minutes to about 2.5 minutes on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU.

Nir Minerbi, Chief Executive Officer of Classiq, said fast test cycles are key for real use. He said the goal is to help teams "move from high-level intent to running experiments faster."

This trend shows how firms are not waiting for large quantum machines. Instead, they are using AI and GPU tools today to accelerate quantum computing.

5. UK Commits $2 billion to Build Quantum Lead

Finally, the United Kingdom has set out a plan to invest up to $2 billion in quantum tech. The goal is to build large systems by the early 2030s and boost growth across key sectors.

The plan includes funding for compute, sensing, and secure data links. It also sets up a new program to test and scale quantum systems through public and private work.

Officials expect the move to support jobs and raise output over time. The program aims to tie research, build, and use into one system. In sum, the step shows how nations now see quantum as a core tech, on par with AI in long term value.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up firms tied to the quantum space. In the list, you will find pure-play firms such as IonQ (IONQ) and Rigetti Computing (RGTI), as well as large tech firms with quantum work, such as Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOGL). The tool provides a clear view of how these firms compare as the space continues to evolve.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Shell Stock (SHEL) Shatters after Iranian Missile Causes 'Extensive Damage' to Qatar Gas Plant
data.description:
Shares in oil giant Shell (SHEL) were lower today after one of its gas plants in Qatar suffered "extensive damage" from an Iranian missile attack.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shell Staff are Safe

Shell said the attack on Ras Laffan Industrial City on the evening of March 18 led to a fire within the Pearl GTL facility. It said the fire was "rapidly extinguished" and that all staff on site are safe. It added that the situation is under control and that the plant is in a safe state.

"We are currently assessing any potential damage to Pearl GTL and working with QatarEnergy and the relevant authorities to understand the damage to the wider Ras Laffan Industrial City facilities," it said in a statement.

However, state-run QatarEnergy was more dramatic declaring that the attack had caused "extensive damage" to the site.

Developed in partnership with QatarEnergy, Pearl Gas-to-Liquids (GTL) is the world's largest GTL plant. Up to 1.6 billion cubic feet per day of wellhead gas from 22 offshore wells is converted to gas-to-liquids using Shell's proprietary Shell Middle Distillate Synthesis (SMDS) process.

The work at the plant involves converting gas into liquid products that include fuel for transport, motor oils and the ingredients for everyday goods such as plastics, detergents and cosmetics.

Gas Prices Soar

Shell has other sites in Qatar, including Qatargas 4, which is a fully integrated liquefied natural gas (LNG) asset with joint ownership by QatarEnergy (70%) and Shell (30%). It comprises offshore production platforms and pipelines; onshore gas processing, treatment and liquefaction facilities.

LNG production in Qatar has been shut down since early March and the worry is that the escalation of the conflict into an 'energy' rather than 'military war' will mean it could be suspended for some time to come.

The attacks led to gas prices soaring to a three-year high and oil prices spiking higher. More action could follow given President Trump's threats to "massively blow up the entirety" of Iran's South Pars gas field if the nation attacks Qatar's facilities again.

Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB, said the escalation in the conflict was "spooking the market". "This war looks far from over, and the energy crisis is shifting from a shipping crisis to a supply crisis," she said.

Is SHEL a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, SHEL has a Moderate Buy consensus based on 5 Buy and 3 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $106. SHEL stock's consensus price target is $89.17, implying a 3.86% downside.
data.author: David Craik
